title: anisomeles antrorsa
intro: Anisomeles antrorsa is a species of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the 'Wild Basil' plant, native to tropical Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Anisomeles antrorsa include treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as aiding in digestion and providing relief from anxiety and stress. In Ayurvedic medicine, the plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed fresh or dried to reap its medicinal benefits.
